de-direct-honesty.observation: Colleague is being direct and critical
de-direct-honesty.culturalFramework: German communication style values directness and honesty. Blunt criticism is not aggression—it signals high engagement and respect. Germans see sugar-coating as disrespectful.
de-direct-honesty.tacticResponse: Don't take offense. This is a sign of professional respect. Respond with equally direct reasoning. Address the specific concern head-on.

de-structured-agenda.culturalFramework: German business culture values clear structure, written agendas, and time management. Deviation from agenda is seen as disrespectful of participants' time.
de-structured-agenda.tacticResponse: Provide written agenda in advance. Stick to time limits. If a new topic arises, propose adding it to next meeting's agenda.

de-evidence-based.observation: Team demands facts and evidence before deciding
de-evidence-based.culturalFramework: German engineering/business culture is evidence-driven. Decisions require data, testing, and logical proof. Intuition or soft arguments are insufficient.
de-evidence-based.tacticResponse: Come prepared with concrete data, case studies, test results. Be specific. Avoid speculation. Show your methodology clearly.

de-decision-clarity.observation: Team wants clear decision ownership and timeline
de-decision-clarity.culturalFramework: German culture expects clarity on decision authority and deadlines. Ambiguous responsibility or indefinite timelines create frustration.
de-decision-clarity.tacticResponse: Clearly state: Who decides? What's the deadline? Make decisions in the meeting or schedule next decision point explicitly.

trigger: we're spending too much time here
de-efficiency-focus.observation: Team is action-oriented and values efficiency
de-efficiency-focus.culturalFramework: German business culture prioritizes efficiency and rapid execution. Long deliberations are seen as inefficient. Decide and move forward quickly.
de-efficiency-focus.tacticResponse: Keep discussions focused and time-bound. Come to conclusions. If more time is needed, explicitly schedule it separately.

de-process-compliance.culturalFramework: German culture respects established processes and rules. Following proper procedure is a sign of professionalism and organizational integrity.
de-process-compliance.tacticResponse: Align with established processes. If suggesting a different approach, justify it clearly and get agreement before proceeding.

de-precision-language.observation: Team is seeking precise, unambiguous language
de-precision-language.culturalFramework: German culture values precise language and clear definitions. Vagueness is seen as unprofessional or evasive.
de-precision-language.tacticResponse: Use exact terms. Define abbreviations. Avoid idioms or ambiguous phrases. Be clinically precise.

de-boundary-respect.observation: Team member is clarifying role boundaries
de-boundary-respect.culturalFramework: German organizational culture has clear role definitions and accountability. Respecting boundaries shows professionalism and prevents scope creep.
de-boundary-respect.tacticResponse: Respect the stated boundary. Route the issue to the appropriate person/team. Avoid asking someone to exceed their role.
